quick_answer_bullets: Drive on the LEFT—take extra care at junctions
Speed limits posted in mph; cameras widely used
London Congestion Charge applies on weekdays
Using a handheld phone while driving is illegal

speed_limits.notes: Built-up areas default to 30 mph unless signed. Dual carriageways 70 mph; single carriageways often 60 mph. Lower limits common near schools.

alcohol_limit.notes: Scotland limit is 0.05% BAC. Penalties are severe—custody and long bans common.

rules.seatbelt_rules: Mandatory for all occupants. Driver responsible for under-14s.
rules.child_seat_rules: Children must use appropriate car seat until 12 years or 135 cm.
rules.phone_usage_rules: Handheld phone use illegal while driving—even when stopped in traffic. Hands-free allowed if not distracting.
rules.headlights_rules: Use headlights at night and in poor visibility. No daytime requirement except in some conditions.
rules.priority_rules: Give way to the right at equal-priority junctions. Roundabouts: give way to traffic from the right already on the circle.

tolls.notes: Most roads are free. Congestion Charge (London), Dartford Crossing, M6 Toll, and some bridges charge fees.

rules.parking_rules: Yellow lines restrict parking. Controlled zones use pay-and-display or apps. Do not park on zig-zag lines near crossings.
rules.winter_rules: No national winter tyre law, but snow chains may be needed in Scottish Highlands when signed.

summary: The UK drives on the left with mph limits and extensive speed-camera enforcement. London adds congestion and ULEZ charges. Roundabouts are common—give way to traffic from the right.
common_traps: Driving on the wrong side after leaving roundabouts or rental-car pickup.
Speed cameras (fixed, mobile, and average-speed) on motorways and urban routes.
London Congestion Charge and ULEZ zones—cameras enforce payment.
Bus lanes and box junctions are camera-enforced in cities.

summaries.title: Speeding
summaries.summary: Fixed, mobile, and average-speed cameras are widespread. Penalties depend on how far over the limit; court for serious cases.
summaries.summary_de: Feste, mobile und Streckenradar-Kameras sind weit verbreitet. Strafen hängen von der Überschreitung ab; Gericht bei schweren Fällen.

summaries.title: Mobile phone while driving
summaries.summary: Any handheld phone use while driving is illegal—including at red lights. Hands-free is permitted if you stay in full control.
summaries.summary_de: Jeder Handheld-Handygebrauch am Steuer ist illegal—auch an roten Ampeln. Freisprech ist erlaubt bei voller Kontrolle.

summaries.title: Red light violation
summaries.summary: Red-light cameras operate at many urban junctions. Running a light typically means 3 points and a fixed penalty.
summaries.summary_de: Rotlichtkameras an vielen Stadt-Kreuzungen. Rotlichtverstoß bedeutet typischerweise 3 Punkte und Bußgeld.

summaries.title: Alcohol offenses
summaries.summary: England/Wales/Northern Ireland limit 0.08% BAC; Scotland 0.05%. Drink-driving leads to arrest, court, and lengthy bans.
summaries.summary_de: England/Wales/Nordirland 0,08 % BAC; Schottland 0,05 %. Trunkenheit am Steuer führt zu Festnahme, Gericht und langem Entzug.

categories.title: Speeding
categories.description: Fixed penalty notices for minor excess; court referral for higher speeds.
categories.description_de: Bußgeldbescheid bei geringer Überschreitung; Gericht bei höheren Geschwindigkeiten.

rows.label: Drug driving (zero tolerance for many substances)
rows.label_de: Drogen am Steuer (Nulltoleranz für viele Substanzen)

rows.label: Not in proper control of vehicle

faq.question: Which side of the road do you drive on in the UK?
faq.question_de: Auf welcher Straßenseite fährt man in Großbritannien?
faq.answer: You drive on the left. Give way to the right at equal-priority junctions and on roundabouts. Take extra care after rental pickup, at one-way systems, and when turning—foreign drivers often drift to the wrong side.
faq.answer_de: Man fährt links. Vorfahrt dem Verkehr von rechts an gleichwertigen Kreuzungen und auf Kreisverkehren. Nach Mietwagenabholung, in Einbahnstraßen und beim Abbiegen besonders aufpassen—Ausländer geraten oft auf die falsche Seite.

faq.question: What are typical speed limits in the UK?
faq.question_de: Was sind typische Tempolimits in Großbritannien?
faq.answer: Limits are in mph: 30 in built-up areas (unless signed), 60 on single carriageways, 70 on dual carriageways and motorways. Many residential areas use 20 mph zones. Always follow posted signs—they override defaults.
faq.answer_de: Limits in mph: 30 in geschlossenen Ortschaften (sofern nicht anders ausgeschildert), 60 auf einspurigen Straßen, 70 auf zweispurigen und Autobahnen. Viele Wohngebiete haben 20-mph-Zonen. Immer Schilder befolgen.

faq.question: How common are speed cameras in the UK?
faq.question_de: Wie verbreitet sind Blitzer in Großbritannien?
faq.answer: Very common. Fixed Gatso/HADECS cameras, mobile vans, and SPECS average-speed zones operate nationwide. Penalty points and fines arrive by post. Sat-nav camera alerts are legal but do not excuse speeding.
faq.answer_de: Sehr verbreitet. Feste Gatso/HADECS-Kameras, mobile Messwagen und SPECS-Streckenradar landesweit. Punkte und Bußgelder per Post. Navi-Blitzerwarnungen sind legal, entschuldigen aber nichts.

faq.question: What is the London Congestion Charge?
faq.question_de: Was ist die London Congestion Charge?
faq.answer: A weekday daytime fee for driving in central London (roughly 7am–6pm). Pay online by midnight the next charging day. Cameras read plates—unpaid charges escalate to penalty notices around £180.
faq.answer_de: Eine werktägliche Tagessgebühr fürs Fahren in Zentrallondon (ca. 7–18 Uhr). Online bis Mitternacht am nächsten Gebührentag zahlen. Kameras lesen Kennzeichen—unbezahlt eskalieren Strafen auf ca. £180.

faq.question: What is the ULEZ in London?
faq.question_de: Was ist die ULEZ in London?
faq.answer: The Ultra Low Emission Zone covers most of Greater London 24/7. Non-compliant cars pay a daily charge or face penalty notices. Check your vehicle's Euro standard on Transport for London before driving in.
faq.answer_de: Die Ultra Low Emission Zone gilt fast ganz London 24/7. Nicht konforme Autos zahlen Tagesgebühr oder erhalten Strafzettel. Euro-Norm auf Transport for London vor der Fahrt prüfen.

faq.question: How do roundabouts work in the UK?
faq.answer: Give way to traffic already on the roundabout from your right. Signal left when exiting. On multi-lane roundabouts, choose your lane early and follow road markings—getting it wrong is a common tourist mistake.
faq.answer_de: Vorfahrt dem Verkehr von rechts, der bereits im Kreisverkehr ist. Blinker links beim Verlassen. Bei mehrspurigen Kreisverkehren früh Spur wählen und Markierungen folgen—häufiger Touristenfehler.

faq.question: Can I use my phone at a red light in the UK?
faq.question_de: Darf ich in Großbritannien bei Rot am Handy sein?
faq.answer: No. Handheld phone use is illegal whenever the engine is running, including in traffic queues and at red lights. Penalty is £200 and 6 points. Hands-free calls are allowed if you remain in proper control.
faq.answer_de: Nein. Handheld-Handygebrauch ist illegal bei laufendem Motor, auch im Stau und an roten Ampeln. Strafe £200 und 6 Punkte. Freisprech ist erlaubt bei voller Kontrolle.

faq.question: Is the drink-drive limit different in Scotland?
faq.question_de: Ist die Promillegrenze in Schottland anders?
faq.answer: Yes. Scotland's limit is 0.05% BAC versus 0.08% in England, Wales, and Northern Ireland. Even one drink can put you over the Scottish limit—plan alternative transport.
faq.answer_de: Ja. Schottlands Grenze ist 0,05 % BAC gegenüber 0,08 % in England, Wales und Nordirland. Schon ein Drink kann reichen—Alternative planen.

faq.question: Can I drive in the UK with a foreign licence?
faq.question_de: Kann ich in Großbritannien mit ausländischem Führerschein fahren?
faq.answer: Visitors may drive on a valid non-UK licence for up to 12 months. Carry your licence and insurance documents. Some non-Latin licences need an IDP. Check DVLA guidance for your country.
faq.answer_de: Besucher dürfen bis zu 12 Monate mit gültigem Nicht-UK-Führerschein fahren. Führerschein und Versicherung mitführen. Manche nicht-lateinischen Scheine brauchen IDP. DVLA-Richtlinien für dein Land prüfen.

faq.question: What should I know when renting a car in the UK?
faq.question_de: Was sollte ich beim Mietwagen in Großbritannien beachten?
faq.answer: Most rentals are manual—request automatic early. Confirm Congestion Charge/ULEZ registration. Drive on the left from the first metre. Narrow rural roads and roundabouts need extra caution.
faq.answer_de: Die meisten Mietwagen haben Schaltgetriebe—Automatik früh anfragen. Congestion Charge/ULEZ-Registrierung klären. Von Meter eins links fahren. Schmale Landstraßen und Kreisverkehre brauchen Vorsicht.

rental_and_idp_notes: Valid licence accepted for visitors (usually 12 months). IDP recommended for non-Latin script licences. Manual transmission is default—book automatic if needed.

tolls.notes: Congestion Charge weekdays; ULEZ 24/7 across most of Greater London.

regional_variations.notes: Congestion Charge, ULEZ, bus lanes, and box junctions are camera-enforced. Avoid driving in the centre if possible.

highlights.title: Congestion Charge zone
highlights.description: Weekday fee for central London driving. Pay online promptly—late payment triggers penalty charge notices.

highlights.title: ULEZ emissions zone
highlights.description: Most of Greater London requires Euro-compliant vehicles or a daily charge. Check before entering with a rental car.

highlights.title: Bus lanes & box junctions
highlights.description: Yellow box junctions and bus lanes are camera-enforced. Do not enter a box unless your exit is clear.

alcohol_limit.notes: Lower limit than England and Wales.

regional_variations.notes: Lower drink-drive limit. Single-track roads with passing places in Highlands. Winter conditions on A9 and mountain routes.

highlights.title: Lower drink-drive limit
highlights.description: Scotland enforces 0.05% BAC—stricter than the rest of the UK. Plan taxis or designated drivers.

highlights.title: Highland single-track roads
highlights.description: Use passing places to let oncoming traffic pass. Reverse to a passing place if the other driver is closer.

highlights.title: Winter driving in Highlands
highlights.description: Snow and ice on A9, A82, and mountain passes. Check Police Scotland travel advice before long journeys.

source_entries.title: GOV.UK - Driving and transport
source_entries.notes: Official UK government portal for driving rules, penalties, and visitor guidance.

source_entries.title: The Highway Code
source_entries.notes: Official rules of the road including signs, roundabouts, and priority.
